title: Cloud Misconfiguration Lessons from Real Incidents
excerpt: Common cloud configuration mistakes and the controls that prevent them.
author: Tony Rogers
content:
## Misconfiguration is still a top risk

Most cloud breaches do not require advanced exploits. They rely on overly permissive access, public storage, and weak key hygiene.

## Practical controls

- Enforce least-privilege IAM by default.
- Block public object storage unless explicitly approved.
- Rotate secrets and use managed identity where possible.
- Alert on changes to network and identity policy.

## Make security continuous

Integrate policy checks into CI pipelines so risky configuration changes fail early before reaching production.
